name: qSOFA
description: Identifies high-risk patients for in-hospital mortality with suspected infection outside the ICU.
purpose: Predict in-hospital mortality in non-ICU patients with confirmed or suspected infection.
when_to_use: Patients ≥18 years old in a non-ICU setting (i.e. pre-hospital, ward, emergency department, or step-down unit) with confirmed or suspected infection.

interpretation.description: Not high risk for in-hospital mortality
High risk for in-hospital mortality (3- to 14-fold increase)

references.citation: Seymour CW, Liu VX, Iwashyna TJ, Brunkhorst FM, Rea TD, Scherag A, Rubenfeld G, Kahn JM, Shankar-Hari M, Singer M, Deutschman CS, Escobar GJ, Angus DC. Assessment of Clinical Criteria for Sepsis: For the Third International Consensus Definitions for Sepsis and Septic Shock (Sepsis-3). JAMA. 2016 Feb 23;315(8):762-774.

notes: qSOFA is a mortality predictor, NOT a diagnostic test for sepsis. A positive qSOFA Score (≥2) should prompt further investigation for organ dysfunction or calculation of a full SOFA score, but should not by itself trigger sepsis-directed interventions like initiation of broad-spectrum antibiotics. The 2021 Surviving Sepsis Campaign guidelines do not integrate qSOFA in recommendations for screening or diagnosis of sepsis. Can be repeated serially if there is a change in clinical condition.
